name: Pumpkin Bread Pudding
recipeInstructions:
Pudding: Beat eggs in a large bowl. Add milk and beat again. Stir in pumpkin, sugar, cinnamon, nutmeg, salt, and vanilla. Add bread pieces and press with your hands to submerge the bread in the milk mixture. Cover and refrigerate several hours or overnight; break up the bread the next day and bake as directed; if not, proceed as follows. Set the mixture aside for 15 minutes. Use your fingers to break up the chunks of bread. Set aside another 15 minutes and repeat. Add raisins. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Generously butter a 2-quart baking dish. Fill the dish with bread pudding mixture and bake for 1 hour, or until set. Serve warm, hot, or cold with hot bourbon sauce.
Bourbon Sauce: Use unsalted butter for the best bourbon sauce; margarine doesn't make a great sauce. Melt butter in a bowl over hot but not boiling water. Combine egg, sugar, and milk in a small bowl and beat until light colored. Add to butter mixture and beat with a whisk over hot water until sugar dissolves (at least 5 minutes). Add bourbon and stir. Remove from stove and serve.
